title: Resource nodes with extraction, depletion, and seasons
description: Implement src/world/resource_node.rs: ResourceNode struct with fields: id, position (Vec2), commodity (one of TIMBER, ORE, GRAIN, FISH, CLAY, HERBS), base_yield (1-5 units per extraction), depletion (f32, 0.0-1.0), currently_harvested (bool flag per tick). Methods: (1) extract(season) -> f32: returns yield adjusted for depletion (at 80% depletion yield drops to 50%, at 100% yields 0) and seasonal modifiers (GRAIN/HERBS ×2 summer ×0.3 winter; FISH ×1.5 spring ×0.5 autumn; CLAY ×0 in winter if in northern 30% of map; TIMBER/ORE unaffected). Extraction takes 3 ticks per unit. (2) tick_regeneration: if not harvested this tick, recover depletion at 0.05%/tick. Exhausted nodes (100%) recover to 20% after 2000 ticks of no extraction. (3) Scatter placement: generate 20-30 nodes avoiding mountains and water, distributed across the map. Ensure at least one of each commodity type exists.
result_summary:
Everything compiles. Here's what was implemented in `src/world/resource_node.rs`:

**`ResourceNode` struct** with fields: `id`, `position` (Vec2), `commodity` (raw tier-0 only), `base_yield` (1-5), `depletion` (0.0-1.0), `currently_harvested`, and internal `ticks_exhausted_idle`.

**Methods:**

- **`extract(season, map_height) -> f32`** — Returns yield adjusted by a piecewise depletion multiplier (100% at 0% depletion → 50% at 80% → 0% at 100%) and seasonal modifiers:
  - GRAIN/HERBS: ×2 summer, ×0.3 winter
  - FISH: ×1.5 spring, ×0.5 autumn
  - CLAY: ×0 winter in northern 30% of map
  - TIMBER/ORE: unaffected
  - Advances depletion proportional to yield extracted

- **`tick_regeneration()`** — If not harvested: recovers 0.05%/tick. Exhausted nodes (100%) snap to 80% depletion (20% capacity) after 2000 idle ticks. Harvesting resets the idle counter.

- **`scatter(count, ...)`** — Rejection-samples passable positions, guarantees at least one of each raw commodity, enforces minimum 6 nodes.

- **`generate(config, ...)`** — Convenience wrapper using `WorldConfig`.

**Constants:** `TICKS_PER_UNIT = 3` (exposed for callers to enforce extraction timing).
